summary.intent: Implement the layered tool policy system described in GitHub discussion #641, which identified that `resolve_effective_policy` was dead code disconnected from both the config schema and runtime, to enable per-channel/per-sender tool gating for mixed-trust environments.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented a 6-layer tool policy system: wired `resolve_effective_policy` into the runtime, extended config schema with per-provider and per-channel policy fields, threaded `sender_id` through all channel handlers and runtime context, added sandbox layer, wrote documentation, and shipped as PR #677 with a comment posted to the original discussion.

repo: resolve_effective_policy in crates/tools/src/policy.rs was previously only called by its own unit tests — the actual runtime used a separate effective_tool_policy() reading only global config
AgentsConfig uses presets: HashMap<String, AgentPreset> (not an array), and AgentPreset already had tools: PresetToolPolicy before this session
ChannelMessageMeta existed but had no sender_id field; peer_id was available in all 6 channel handlers (Telegram, Slack, MSTeams, Discord, Matrix, WhatsApp) but not threaded through
PromptHostRuntimeContext already had channel_type, channel_account_id, channel_chat_id, channel_chat_type — sender_id was the only missing piece
The worktree was missing biome.json (added to main after the branch diverged), causing biome lint failures that were resolved by merging origin/main
local-validate.sh 677 is the standard PR validation script; CUDA-related build failures for llama-cpp are expected on macOS and pre-existing
Clippy is configured with -D warnings, enforcing field_reassign_with_default: must use struct initialization with ..Default::default() instead of post-construction field assignment

code.finding: Clippy field_reassign_with_default error: must use ProviderEntry { policy: Some(...), ..Default::default() } not let mut x = Default::default(); x.field = ...
Test constructs PromptHostRuntimeContext without ..Default::default(), so every new field must be explicitly added to the test initializer
build_policy_context() had sandboxed: false hardcoded — needed to read from runtime_context.sandbox.exec_sandboxed to make layer 6 reachable
apply_runtime_tool_filters has 4 call sites: list_tools schema path, raw_prompt non-streaming send, full_context streaming send, and run_with_tools
build_schema_map() must be updated whenever new config fields are added to schema structs, otherwise the config validator rejects them

workflow: Beads issues were created first, then claimed before starting work — the project uses a claim/close workflow for task tracking
Background agents (subagent_type=general-purpose) were used in parallel for independent subtasks (docs page, sandbox policy field) while the main thread handled sender_id threading
cargo fmt --all must be run before committing; the project uses nightly-2025-11-30 for formatting
local-validate.sh <PR-number> is the full pre-push validation script covering biome, i18n parity, cargo fmt, install name checks, zizmor, lint, and tests
GitHub Discussions comments require GraphQL API (addDiscussionComment mutation) — the REST API does not support posting discussion comments

friction: biome.json was missing from the worktree (added to main after branch diverged) — caused spurious biome CI failures requiring an origin/main merge to resolve
sandboxed: false was hardcoded in build_policy_context(), making layer 6 silently dead — caught only on second review pass
Two post-push compilation errors required hotfix commits: missing channel_sender_id in prompt.rs test initializer, and Clippy field_reassign_with_default in policy.rs test
CUDA/llama-cpp build failures on macOS pollute cargo check output, requiring --exclude flags to get clean signal on changed crates

channel_binding_from_runtime_context builds ChannelBinding for hook payloads but sender_id was only accessible via params (not runtime context), requiring adding channel_sender_id to PromptHostRuntimeContext as an intermediate carrier
open_items: sender_id in channel_binding_from_runtime_context for hook payloads depends on #640 (channel metadata in hook payloads) — the policy path works but hooks still have a gap
A dedicated docs/src/tool-policy.md page was created but a more comprehensive treatment could cover migration from the old effective_tool_policy() pattern
SandboxConfig.tools_policy was added (layer 6) but the sandbox exec_sandboxed flag plumbing to all downstream contexts should be audited — only the policy path was verified
No dedicated tool-policy docs page existed before this session; the new page covers 5 layers in its initial version but was patched to add layer 6 after the sandbox agent completed
